title: leptinella pectinata subsp. villosa
intro: Leptinella pectinata subsp. villosa, also known as hair-leaved leptinella or hairy leptinella, is a small, hairless to hairy perennial daisy-like plant native to New Zealand and Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine, and its leaves contain sesquiterpenes, which have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, providing potential medicinal benefits. The health benefits of Leptinella pectinata subsp. villosa include its potential to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and oxidative stress. Leptinella pectinata subsp. villosa can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and optimal dosage.
